description: Autonomous, Persistent RPG World Engine — distributed-first layout separating machine logic from narrative lore.

data.description: Hybrid Storage Layer — the single source of truth for all world state and lore.

lore.description: Human-readable narrative (Markdown). Indexed into ChromaDB for RAG retrieval.

core.description: Canonical world truth. Maintained by Core team only. Read-only for community contributions.
core.codex: World history, cosmology, locations, and primary faction lore.
core.sessions: Official session transcripts and turn logs.

community.description: Community lore packs. Segregated per author handle. Lower RAG priority than core/.

<author-handle>.community.json: Pack manifest — declares all new content and references.

state.description: Machine-readable current world state (JSON). Validated against /schemas/ on every write.

core.description: Primary world state. Only writable by Core MCP servers with authorization token.
core.entities: NPCs, player characters, enemies, and their live attributes.
core.factions: Faction standings, resources, and player relationship scores.

community.description: Community-contributed state extensions. Cannot overwrite core fields.

infrastructure.description: Node Backbone (The Brawn) — databases, network config, and migration scripts.
infrastructure.docker-compose.yml: Spins up PostgreSQL 16 and ChromaDB with Tailscale mesh binding.
infrastructure..env.example: Environment variable template. Copy to .env and fill in credentials.

migrations.description: SQL scripts auto-executed by PostgreSQL on fresh boot (initdb.d mount).
migrations.001_init_world_state.sql: Creates core entity, faction, and session tables.

tailscale.description: Tailscale ACL policies and mesh configuration for split-node deployments.
tailscale.acl.json: Access control list — restricts DB ports to Tailscale mesh only.

mcp-servers.description: The Bridge — Model Context Protocol servers connecting the Inference Node to the Infrastructure Node.

fs-manager.description: Zero-Touch file handler. Validates all writes against JSON Schema before executing CRUD on /data.
fs-manager.server.py: FastAPI MCP server exposing apply_world_update and read_state tools.
fs-manager.validator.py: JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 validation layer — the gatekeeper.
fs-manager.tests: Automated test suite covering schema rejection, path traversal prevention, and successful writes.

db-vector.description: RAG/DB interface — routes semantic queries to ChromaDB and structured queries to PostgreSQL.
db-vector.server.py: MCP server exposing query_lore, upsert_entity, and search_context tools.
db-vector.tests: Test suite for vector query accuracy and DB connection resilience.

git-sync.description: Automated version control — commits after each world update with session metadata.
git-sync.tests: Test suite for commit generation and rollback recovery.

schemas.description: Shared JSON Schema contracts (Draft 2020-12) validating all MCP tool payloads and state files.
schemas.apply_world_update.schema.json: Strict input schema for the fs-manager's primary write tool.
schemas.entity.schema.json: Schema for NPC/player entity state objects.

world-engine.description: Inference & Orchestration (The Brain) — LLM agents and the core turn-processing loop.

agents.description: Prompt boundaries and persona definitions for each specialized agent.
agents.dm.yaml: Dungeon Master — storyteller, rule arbiter, narrative generator.
agents.fact-extractor.yaml: Parses DM narrative into structured <world_update> JSON events.
agents.lorekeeper.yaml: Manages RAG context injection — queries ChromaDB, prioritizes Core over Community.

orchestrator.description: The Core Loop — routes player input through the full Action → Narrative → Extract → Update pipeline.
orchestrator.main.py: Primary event loop. Entry point for the Inference Node.

simulation.description: Background world progression — cron-driven events that mutate world state without player input.
simulation.scheduler.py: Time-based simulation tick (faction movements, weather changes, NPC actions).

ISSUE_TEMPLATE.lore_entry.md: Lore-Smith template for new NPCs, locations, and codex entries.
ISSUE_TEMPLATE.mcp_tool.md: Technician template for new Python MCP server tools.
ISSUE_TEMPLATE.schema_refinement.md: Architect template for JSON state model updates.

.github.PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md: Standard PR template enforcing contributor pathway declaration.

root.README.md: Project overview, architecture diagram, and bootstrap instructions.
root.CONTRIBUTING.md: Contributor pathways (Lore-Smith, Schema-Architect, Technician) and coding standards.
root.ARCHITECTURE.md: Core vs. Community framework, namespace separation, override hierarchy, and protected fields.
root.folder_structure.json: This file — machine-readable repo manifest for agent and contributor tooling.
